The vegetation (large deciduous broadleaf trees) and the specific style of lush, manicured green grass are inconsistent with the majority of Australian suburban landscapes, which typically feature different flora (e.g., eucalyptus) and different soil/climate profiles.

While Australia has similar suburban lawns and temperate zones, the specific morphology of the large deciduous tree in the center is more typical of North American species (like Quercus or Acer) than native Australian flora, though introduced species exist there.

The combination of the specific white plastic mailbox on a post, the red brick and vinyl siding construction of the suburban homes, and the overall Google Street View image quality is highly characteristic of the United States. The mailbox style is a quintessential US suburban meta detail.
